zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean Ier de Bruyères於1228年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌布盧艾雷勒-a特爾時，他的父親Thomas III de Bruyères28歲，母親Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais17歲。他於1274年與Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix在法國豪-德-福朗斯奧易相利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1298年享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean Ier de Bruyères于1228年出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌布盧艾雷勒-a特爾时，他的父亲Thomas III de Bruyères28岁，母亲Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais17岁。他于1274年与Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix在法国豪-德-福朗斯奧易相利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1298年享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bruyères
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel.

French (Bruyère): from Old French bruiere ‘heather’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived in a place where heather grew, or a habitational name from one of the places in France (for example in Calvados) named with this word. Compare Labruyere .
Altered form (Bruyère) of French Bruguier: habitational name from (Le) Bruguier, the name of several places in the southeastern part of France, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Labruyere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jean-Philippe.

French (Labruyère): variant, with fused feminine definite article la, of Bruyère ‘heather’ (see Bruyere 1), a topographic name for someone who lived in a place where heather grew, or a habitational name from one of the places in France named La Bruyère.
Altered form (Labruyère) of French Labrière, a cognate of 1 above (see Labrier ).

variants.name: Briere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Pierre, Adrien, Aurele, Benedicte, Benoit, Colette, Fernand, Gaston, Marcel, Micheline.

French (Brière): topographic name from a regional variant of Old French bruiere ‘heather’ (see Bruyere ), or a habitational name from La Brière, the name of several places mainly in the northern part of France, from the same word.
History: Jean Brière from Clarbec in Calvados, France, married Jeanne Grandin in Quebec City, QC, in 1671.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Yiddish ber ‘bear’, probably influenced by the early medieval European practice of giving animal names to people. It is often paired with Dov in order to provide a Hebrew name in certain rituals.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean Ier de Bruyères was born in 1228, in Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, his father, Thomas III de Bruyères, was 28 and his mother, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, was 17. He married Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix in 1274, in Chantilly, Oise, Hauts-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in 1298, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean Ier de Bruyères est né en 1228, à Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Thomas III de Bruyères, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix en 1274, à Chantilly, Oise, Hauts-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1298, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean Ier de Bruyères 1228 in Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas III de Bruyères 28 und seine Mutter Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais 17. Er hat 1274 in Chantilly, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1298 im Alter von 70 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Ier de Bruyères è nato il nel 1228 nel Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Thomas III de Bruyères, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix nel 1274, nel Chantilly, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1298, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean Ier de Bruyèresが1228年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスエソンブルイeレス-レ-チaテルで生まれた時，父Thomas III de Bruyèresは28歳，母Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinaisは17歳であった。1274年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスオイゼチャンティリーでEustachie de Lévis-Mirepoixと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1298年に70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean Ier de Bruyères(이)가 1228년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 에손 브루이e레스-레-cha텔에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas III de Bruyères(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1274년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 이스 챈틸리에서 Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1298년 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Ier de Bruyères nasceu em 1228, em Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Thomas III de Bruyères, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix em 1274, em Chantilly, Oise, Hauts-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1298, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean Ier de Bruyères родился в 1228 г. в местности бруиeрес-ле-чaтел, эссонн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Thomas III de Bruyères было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix в 1274 г. в местности чантилли, оис, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1298 г. в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean Ier de Bruyères nació en 1228, en Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Thomas III de Bruyères, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Eustachie de Lévis-Mirepoix en 1274, en Chantilly, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1298, a la edad de 70 años.
